trails.name: Coach Stream Route
trails.description: Follow the beginning of the old coach road.

trails.name: Sth Face Tk - Staveley R to Bus Stp jnct
Sth Face Tk - Woolshed CP to Bus Stp jnc
Camp Saddle Track
trails.description: An access track leads to Camp Saddle. From here, experienced backcountry trampers can explore untracked terrain.

trails.name: Hut Creek Track
trails.description: Links Broken River Ski-field Road to the Bridge Hill Road.

trails.name: Frew Hut to Frew Saddle Track
Frew Saddle to Bluff Hut Track
Neave Hut to Price Flat Hut Track
Price Flat Hut to Frew Hut Track
Pinnacles Hut to Sharplin Falls Carpark
Woolshed Creek Hut (inc) to Pinnacles Ht
Woolshed Ck Crpk to Woolshed Creek Hut
Sth Face Tk - Sharplin CP to Staveley R
Helicopter Hill Track
trails.description: This track leads from Lyndon Saddle to the summit of Helicopter Hill, with great views.

trails.name: Trig M Route
trails.description: Follow an old 4WD track to a high point above Lake Lyndon.

trails.name: Scotts Saddle Track
trails.description: Starting in Mt Hutt Forest and ending at Mount Hutt Skifield Road, Scotts Saddle Track offers beautiful views over mid-Canterbury.

trails.name: Craigieburn Edge Track
trails.description: This track follows the Craigieburn Valley through mountain beech forest and tussock. It is best walked in summer and early autumn, as there can be avalanches when snow is present on the hills.

trails.name: Orari Gorge Track
trails.description: This circular walk passes through regenerating forest with some large tōtara, kahikatea and mataī.

trails.name: Pudding Hill Stm Route, Mt Hutt Forest
Deer Spur to Little Mt Peel Tk
Bealey Bridge to Anti Crow Hut Track
Cass - Lagoon Saddle Track Coral Lynn Ro
Cass Lagoon Saddle Track- Lagoon Saddle
Cass Lagoon Saddle Track: SH73 to Cass
Harper Valley Track
trails.description: This track leads up the Harper Valley. This track is part of Te Araroa Trail.

trails.name: Anti Crow Hut to Carrington Hut Track
Bealey Spur Track
trails.description: Experience stunning views in fine weather from a more gradual track than most alpine tracks around Arthur's Pass village.

trails.name: Mistletoe Track
trails.description: This track climbs from the Craigieburn picnic area to Lyndon Saddle

trails.name: Dracophyllum Flat Track
trails.description: This track leads from Jacks Pass to Dracophyllum Flat, a popular picnic spot, then on to Texas Flat

trails.name: Acland Falls Track
trails.description: This track takes you to the 14 m high Acland Falls from Clarke Flat or Te Wanahu Flat.

trails.name: Poet Hut to Top Toaroha Hut Track
Top Toaroha Hut to Cedar Flat Hut Track
Bluff Hut to Poet Hut Track
T.A. (Comyns Hut to Clent H Saddle)
Paddle Creek to Boundary Hut Track
T.A. Clent Hills Saddle-Double Hut Junct
Te Araroa Double Hut to Lake Emily
Potts Hut Track
trails.description: A track to Potts Hut through tussock-covered terraces.

trails.name: Mystery Lake Track
trails.description: The area around Mystery Lake has steep rocky ‘knobs’ smoothed by passing glaciers, jumbled scree and large Hall’s tōtara.

trails.name: Lake Emma to Lake Camp Track
trails.description: Walk or mountain bike to an old musterers hut near Lake Emma.

trails.name: Coal Pit Spur Track
trails.description: This shared-use track links Craigieburn valley and Flock Hill, via Coal Pit Spur.

trails.name: Cuckoo Creek Track
trails.description: This shared-use track links Dracophyllum Flat Track with Cheeseman Road.

trails.name: Cockayne Alley Track
trails.description: This track links Dracophyllum Flat Track with Cheeseman Road.

trails.name: Water Intake Track
trails.description: This track links Water Intake Road to Hogs Back Track (Easy Up)

trails.name: Thomas River Track
trails.description: From the Water Intake Road to Castle Hill Village

trails.name: Leith Hill Loop Track
trails.description: Loop track from/to Castle Hill Village.

trails.name: Bridge Hill Track
trails.description: A short walking track with stunning views from the top of Bridge Hill.

trails.name: Te Kiekie Route
trails.description: This route links the summit of Mount Somers with the Mount Somers Track on the north side of the mountain.

trails.name: Carrington Hut Route
trails.description: This route up the braided riverbed of the Waimakariri leads to a comfortable hut situated below the park’s highest peaks. Carrington Hut is a good base for exploring the upper Waimakariri catchment.

trails.name: Cave Stream Scenic Reserve tracks and cave walk
trails.description: Two short walks lead to the cave entrances – you can can also walk through the cave passage which meanders and twists in pitch darkness for 594 m between the two entrances.

trails.name: Clarke Flat & Te Wanahu Flat tracks
trails.description: Discover a variety of vegetation and a waterfall on these walks - shorter options are suitable for children.

trails.name: Dennistoun Bush Track
trails.description: Walk through magnificent ancient forest on this flat, easy walk.

trails.name: Double Hill access tracks
trails.description: These easements tracks provide access to the Rakaia Valley side of Hakatere Conservation Park.

trails.name: Emily Falls and Rata Falls Tracks
trails.description: Choose between these two waterfall walks.

trails.name: Lake Clearwater tracks
trails.description: Enjoy views of Lake Clearwater from the lake shore or the nearby Mount Guy (1319 m).

trails.name: Lake Emma to Lake Camp Track
trails.description: Walk or mountain bike to an old musterers hut near Lake Emma.

trails.name: Lake Heron tracks
trails.description: Explore the area around Lake Heron on these tracks - discover kettle holes, and views of the lake and surrounding mountains.

trails.name: Little Mt Peel/Huatekerekere tracks
trails.description: Climb one of the most popular peaks in Canterbury for magnificent views to Mt Somers, Mt Hutt and across the plains to the coast.

trails.name: Mount Barrosa Track
trails.description: Climb to the top of Mount Barrosa (1364 m) for good views of the surrounding countryside.

trails.name: Mount Somers summit
trails.description: Climb to the summit of Mount Somers for 360 degrees views.

trails.name: Nature Trail
trails.description: Enjoy a short walk alongside Woolshed Creek.

trails.name: Peak Hill Route
trails.description: The climb to the top of Peak Hill rewards walkers with sweeping views of Lake Coleridge and its surrounds. The walk is extremely exposed to the weather and walkers should be well equipped.

trails.name: Pudding Hill Stream Route
trails.description: This route winds up steeply through Pudding Hill Stream through Mount Hutt Forest.

trails.name: Rakaia Gorge Walkway
trails.description: This walkway offers several geological and historic features as it traverses the edge of the Rakaia Gorge.

trails.name: Kura Tāwhiti Access Track
trails.description: Wander through majestic limestone rock formations of special significance to Ngāi Tahu. It's one of our best short walks.

trails.name: Mt Alford Track
trails.description: Passing through Alford Scenic Reserve, this track takes you to the summit of Mt Alford. From the summit there are wide views across the Canterbury Plains and the inland mountains.

trails.name: Tenehaun Conservation Area tramping tracks
trails.description: The Tenehaun Conservation Area includes walks in the Moorhouse Range and Rangitata Gorge. The Moorhouse Range can be mountain biked along the easement only.

trails.name: Mount Hutt Forest walking tracks
trails.description: Mount Hutt Forest offers three short walking tracks.

trails.name: Annavale Track
trails.description:
A scenic track through short tussock grassland with associated shrubs, to the summit of the Sugarloaf. Most of the track is on a 4WD track which is steep in some places.
Good 360 degrees view covering Thirteen Mile Bush and the Torlesse Range foothills to the plains.

trails.name: Dry Acheron Track
trails.description: Access to Dry Acheron Stream and the two covenant areas is provided courtesy of the Dry Acheron and Big Ben station owners.
